articles.title: Why Ferrari's drivers remain optimistic about their chances
articles.summary: Why Ferrari's drivers remain optimistic about their chances. Having finished third and fourth in the Australian season opener, as well as challenged race winner George Russell, Ferrari drivers Lewis Hamilton and Charles Leclerc have reason to be optimistic this weekend in China.

articles.title: Russell taking 'nothing for granted' ahead of Chinese GP
articles.summary: Russell taking 'nothing for granted' ahead of Chinese GP. George Russell expects Ferrari to be Mercedes' closest competitor this weekend in the Chinese Grand Prix, and admits he is taking "nothing for granted" despite a strong start to the 2026 campaign.

articles.title: McLaren need to 'step it up as much as possible' – Norris
articles.summary: McLaren need to 'step it up as much as possible' – Norris. Reigning Formula 1 World Champion Lando Norris admits that McLaren need to "step it up as much as possible" ahead of this weekend's Chinese Grand Prix.

articles.title: Piastri outlines how he bounced back from home race crash

articles.summary: Watch Weekend Warm-Up ahead of the Chinese GP. After an eventful first round, there are plenty of talking points ahead of the Chinese Grand Prix, and the F1 TV crew are here to run through them all.

articles.title: Norris doesn’t see an unfair advantage in Mercedes PU
articles.summary: Norris doesn’t see an unfair advantage in Mercedes PU. Lando Norris says McLaren is confident that Mercedes is not breaking any rules by retaining more knowledge about how to operate its power unit at the start of the 2026 season.

articles.summary: 'Pretty nuts' to be in podium fight early on debut - Lindblad. Racing Bulls newcomer Arvid Lindblad was fighting as high as third on the opening lap of his maiden F1 start.

articles.summary: Lawson says Perez still not over 2024 rivalry after Australian GP scrap. Liam Lawson feels like Sergio Perez still hasn't got over their 2024 clashes – which culminated in Lawson replacing Perez at Red Bull – following a resumption of their battles in Melbourne.

articles.summary: When is racing not racing? F1's new regulations deliver a split verdict. Chris Medland explores whether F1 racing is now too artificial, and whether drivers' opinions about driving satisfaction are worth considering.

articles.title: Aston Martin encouraged by Honda's progress on vibration issues
articles.summary: Aston Martin encouraged by Honda's progress on vibration issues. After Honda saw progress with its vibration issues, Aston Martin is confident it could have finished the Australian Grand Prix if it wasn’t managing its battery stock.

articles.title: Russell accuses Ferrari of selfishness over rules
articles.summary: Russell accuses Ferrari of selfishness over rules. Mercedes driver George Russell accuses Ferrari of being "selfish" for blocking a change to the rules to improve safety at race starts.
